---
title: "Longitudinal Panel: Slopegraph"
author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: "../1_data/master_macro_panel.csv"
  
  # Variables
  entity_var: "district"
  time_var: "year"
  value_var: "literacy_rate"
  
  # Time Parameters (The two anchor points to compare)
  start_time: 2021
  end_time: 2025
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Literacy Rate (%)"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.path = "../4_output/figures/",
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

# Load data
raw_data <- read_csv(params$data_path)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
# Define string parameters OUTSIDE the mutate chain to prevent vector duplication
start_str <- as.character(params$start_time)
end_str <- as.character(params$end_time)

plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for the specific variables required
  drop_na(!!sym(params$time_var), !!sym(params$entity_var), !!sym(params$value_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Type-Safe Filtering: Convert data to character to match our safe parameters
  mutate(Time_Raw = as.character(!!sym(params$time_var))) %>%
  filter(Time_Raw %in% c(start_str, end_str)) %>%
  
  # 3. Scrub and wrap Entity names
  mutate(
    Entity = str_wrap(str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$entity_var)))), width = 15),
    Value = as.numeric(!!sym(params$value_var)),
    # Lock factor levels so start time is ALWAYS on the left
    Time = factor(Time_Raw, levels = c(start_str, end_str))
  )

# 4. Isolate data for direct left and right text labeling to avoid using a messy Y-axis
left_labels <- plot_data %>% filter(Time == start_str)
right_labels <- plot_data %>% filter(Time == end_str)
```

## Longitudinal Ranking and Trajectory

```{r plot-slopegraph}
source("../2_R/2.0_setup_theme.R", local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Time, y = Value, group = Entity)) +
  
  # Draw the connecting lines
  geom_line(color = cerp_cols["neutral"], linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Draw the anchor points at start and end
  geom_point(color = cerp_cols["primary"], size = 3.5) +
  
  # Direct Left Labels: District Name + Value
  geom_text(data = left_labels, 
            aes(label = paste0(Entity, " (", Value, ")")), 
            hjust = 1.1, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5, color = cerp_cols["dark"]) +
  
  # Direct Right Labels: Just the Value
  geom_text(data = right_labels, 
            aes(label = Value), 
            hjust = -0.3, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5, color = cerp_cols["dark"]) +
  
  # Expand limits horizontally so the text does not get cut off by the edge of the image
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.4, 0.2))) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip the Y-axis entirely for maximum data-ink ratio
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = paste("Shift in", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = paste("Tracking regional trajectories from", params$start_time, "to", params$end_time, "."),
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
  )
```
